title: Day 5: revision_hash Cursor + Stale Response Protocol
description: Extend the fab_get_rules MCP tool with the revision_hash cursor mechanism to solve multi-client phantom cache (state tear). The tool must now: (1) read .fabric/agents.meta.json.revision and return it as revision_hash in every response; (2) accept an optional client_hash parameter; (3) if client_hash is provided and does not match current revision, return stale:true; if matches or not provided, return stale:false. This enables clients to detect when their cached rules are outdated. Manual two-terminal test verifies the mechanism works end-to-end.

files.change: Full implementation: read .fabric/agents.meta.json; load L0 + matching L1/L2 AGENTS.md content for requested path; return { revision_hash, stale, rules: { L0, L1, L2, human_locked_nearby } }; accept path and optional client_hash params
Delegate to .intent-ledger.jsonl append logic (same as CLI ledger-append but callable via MCP); accept entry: { intent, affected_paths }; auto-set timestamp; return success confirmation
Handle add-node / remove-node / update-node ops on .fabric/agents.meta.json; validate op schema with zod; update file and recompute revision hash; return updated revision_hash

files.target: Update server entry to use actual tool implementations
files.change: Replace empty tool stubs with imports from tools/get-rules, tools/append-intent, tools/update-registry; maintain McpServer + StdioServerTransport structure
readAgentsMeta(projectRoot): AgentsMeta; getRevision(projectRoot): string; resolve project root from MCP server working directory

Step-by-step: Terminal A: start Fabric server; Terminal B: call fab_get_rules to get revision_hash; Terminal A: modify L1 AGENTS.md + run fab sync-meta; Terminal B: call fab_get_rules with old hash — must receive stale:true

implementation: Create packages/server/src/meta-reader.ts: readAgentsMeta reads and parses .fabric/agents.meta.json from project root; getRevision returns agents.meta.json.revision; resolves project root from process.cwd() or MCP server startup arg

Implement tools/get-rules.ts: define zod schema for { path: string, client_hash?: string }; load L0 AGENTS.md content; resolve matching L1/L2 nodes from agents.meta.json by matching scope_glob against requested path; load their content; check client_hash against current revision — set stale:true if mismatch; return full response object matching §4.3 TypeScript interface from mcp-first-fabric.md
Implement tools/append-intent.ts: define zod schema for entry input; open .intent-ledger.jsonl in append mode; write JSON line; return { success: true, timestamp }

Implement tools/update-registry.ts: define zod schema for op input (add-node | remove-node | update-node); read current agents.meta.json; apply operation to nodes map; recompute revision hash (sha256 of all node hashes); write updated file; return { revision_hash: newRevision }
Update packages/server/src/index.ts to import actual tool implementations from tools/ directory; replace skeleton handlers; verify all output goes to process.stderr only
Write docs/day5-stale-test.md with exact two-terminal test procedure; include expected response bodies at each step
Run manual stale test per the doc; record actual vs expected response

reference.pattern: MCP tool implementation with zod validation; revision_hash cursor pattern for cache invalidation
reference.examples: mcp-first-fabric.md §4.3 for exact response interface; §3 for revision_hash rationale (Phantom cache risk); McpServer.tool() registration API for zod-validated inputs

criteria: fab_get_rules returns revision_hash field in every response (non-null, sha256: prefixed string from agents.meta.json.revision)
fab_get_rules with no client_hash returns stale:false
fab_get_rules with client_hash matching current revision returns stale:false; with non-matching client_hash returns stale:true
Two-terminal manual test: Terminal A runs fab sync-meta after modifying L1 AGENTS.md; Terminal B's subsequent fab_get_rules with old hash receives stale:true in response
fab_update_registry add-node operation adds node to agents.meta.json and returns new revision_hash; subsequent fab_get_rules reflects the new node

rationale.chosen_approach: Read-only cursor mechanism (client sends hash, server compares) — no server-push needed; consistent with MCP request-response model
alternatives_considered: Server-push/subscription (not supported by MCP protocol spec as of 2026-04)
Polling endpoint separate from fab_get_rules (unnecessary complexity)
File watcher in server (complex, not needed for MVP)
decision_factors: MCP protocol is request-response only — no server-push (locked in synthesis.json parked_ideas)
revision_hash is already computed by fab sync-meta, so server just reads it
Single hash covers entire AGENTS.md tree; clients don't need per-file hashes for cache invalidation
rationale.tradeoffs: Client must re-call fab_get_rules on stale response to get fresh content (two round trips on stale); acceptable for local stdio MCP where latency is negligible

manual_checks: Call fab_get_rules without client_hash — response must contain revision_hash and stale:false
Call fab_get_rules with client_hash matching current revision — stale:false
Call fab_get_rules with client_hash set to 'sha256:invalid' — stale:true
Modify any AGENTS.md file; run fab sync-meta; call fab_get_rules with pre-modification hash — stale:true
Call fab_update_registry with add-node op; call fab_get_rules — new node appears in rules.L1 or L2
success_metrics: revision_hash present in 100% of fab_get_rules responses
stale:true returned within 1 tool call after any AGENTS.md modification + sync-meta
0 stdout writes from server process (all logging to stderr)

risks.description: MCP server needs to know project root to read .fabric/agents.meta.json; path resolution when server is started from different directory
risks.mitigation: Accept --project-root CLI arg in server startup; fallback to process.cwd(); document in README
risks.fallback: Read from environment variable FABRIC_PROJECT_ROOT as alternative to arg

interfaces.name: GetRulesResponse
interfaces.definition: { revision_hash: string; stale: boolean; rules: { L0: string; L1: Array<{path: string; content: string}>; L2: Array<{path: string; content: string}>; human_locked_nearby: Array<{file: string; excerpt: string}> } }
interfaces.purpose: Exact response shape for fab_get_rules per mcp-first-fabric.md §4.3

key_functions.signature: handleGetRules(input: { path: string; client_hash?: string }, projectRoot: string): Promise<GetRulesResponse>
key_functions.purpose: Core logic for fab_get_rules: load content, check staleness, return response

key_functions.signature: resolveRulesForPath(path: string, meta: AgentsMeta, projectRoot: string): Promise<RulesBundle>
key_functions.purpose: Match path against scope_globs in meta.nodes; load matched AGENTS.md content
